惯.origin: A heart 忄 that does the same thing again and again — a habit.

sentence.en: He has the habit of getting up early.

惹.origin: Stirring up the heart 心 of others — to provoke or annoy.

愁.origin: An autumn 秋 heart 心 grows sad — to worry.

sentence.en: He is worried about the exam.

愈.origin: The heart 心 gets better — to heal or recover.

愚.origin: A slow 禺 heart 心 — foolish or silly.

感.origin: Something that touches 咸 the heart 心 — to feel.

愣.origin: A heart 忄 caught off guard — dazed or stunned.

sentence.en: He sits there in a daze.

愧.origin: A heart 忄 haunted like a ghost 鬼 — ashamed.

慕.origin: A heart ⺗ that longs for something — to admire.

憋.origin: A heart 心 held tightly inside — to hold back or stifle.

憎.origin: A heart 忄 that turns away again 曾 — to hate.

憨.origin: A heart 心 that is simple and honest — silly in a sweet way.

sentence.en: He smiles in a simple, sweet way.

懒.origin: A heart 忄 that does not want to move — lazy.

戌.origin: One of the twelve earthly branches, linked to the dog hour.

word.en: a year in the old calendar

sentence.en: This year is the year of Xu.

戎.origin: A hand holding a lance 戈 — weapons and war.

戏.origin: A hand 又 playing with a spear 戈 — a play or game.

戒.origin: Two hands 廾 holding up a spear 戈 to warn — to guard against.

或.origin: Guarding a place 口 with a spear 戈 — 'or, or else'.

sentence.en: You eat an apple or a pear.

戗.origin: To prop up something from the side, like a brace.

戚.origin: Sorrow over a brother lost in war 戈 — also means relatives.

截.origin: A weapon 戈 cutting something off — to cut or stop.

戳.origin: To jab with a spear 戈 — to poke or stab.

sentence.en: He pokes me with his finger.

户.origin: A single door swinging on its hinge — a door or household.

sentence.en: Every household turns on its lights.

扁.origin: A door-shaped board that is thin and wide — flat.

扇.origin: A feathered 羽 thing shaped like a door 户 — a fan.

扎.origin: A hand 扌 tying a bundle with string 乚 — to bind or prick.

sentence.en: The nurse gives me an injection.

扔.origin: Using the hand 扌 to send something away — to throw.

托.origin: Using both hands 扌 to hold something up — to support.

sentence.en: He holds up the bowl with his hand.

扛.origin: Hard work 工 done by hand 扌 — to carry on the shoulder.

sentence.en: He carries a bag of rice on his shoulder.

扣.origin: A hand 扌 fastening or holding back — to button or detain.

执.origin: A hand 扌 firmly holding something — to grasp or carry out.

sentence.en: He carries out the task seriously.

扬.origin: A hand 扌 lifting something into the air — to wave or raise.

sentence.en: The flags flutter in the wind.

扭.origin: A hand 扌 turning something around — to twist.

扮.origin: A hand 扌 making someone look different — to dress up.

扯.origin: A hand 扌 pulling hard — to tear or yank.

扰.origin: A hand 扌 poking at someone — to disturb.

扳.origin: A hand 扌 pulling something back 反 — to pull or pry.

sentence.en: He fixes the car with a wrench.

扶.origin: A hand 扌 helping a person stand — to support.

sentence.en: I support Grandma as she walks.

扼.origin: A hand 扌 gripping tightly — to clutch or grip.

承.origin: Hands lifting and holding something up — to bear or accept.

sentence.en: He is willing to take responsibility.

技.origin: A skill done with the hand 扌 — ability or technique.

抄.origin: A hand 扌 copying words down — to copy.

抒.origin: A hand 扌 giving 予 out feelings — to express.

sentence.en: This song is full of feeling.

抖.origin: A hand 扌 shaking something — to tremble or shake out.

sentence.en: He is shivering from the cold.

抛.origin: A hand 扌 tossing something far away — to throw or abandon.

抠.origin: A hand 扌 picking at something — to dig out with fingers.

sentence.en: Don't pick at the painting on the wall.

抡.origin: A hand 扌 swinging something around — to brandish.

sentence.en: He swings up the big hammer.

抢.origin: A hand 扌 grabbing fast — to snatch or rush.

披.origin: To put on 扌 something over the shoulders like fur 皮 — to drape.

sentence.en: She drapes a coat over her shoulders.

抬.origin: Hands 扌 lifting something up together — to carry or raise.

sentence.en: Please raise your head and look at the board.
